having it's own rolling stock scat

But the Northern Pacific, Great
Northern and Union Pacific, whose
lines are supposed to serve the
territory now in the grip of fuel
famine, claim they cannot furnish
the cars. They are not delivering
coal from their own mines.


When three great railway com
panies confess themselves unable
to meet an emergency situation of
this importance there certainly
seems to be something wrong. Per.
haps the Spokane dealer is right
Here is what Nelson Martin, of the
Rock Springs Fuel Co., says:
